flags.concern: Timeline signatures: projecting grouped clip positions inside `assembleTimelineData()` would leave `resolvedConfig`, preview state, and `signature` stale unless the plan also rebuilds them from the projected config.
flags.evidence: Revised plan addresses 18 open flags by: (1) moving `projectPinnedShotGroups` upstream into `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` so `resolvedConfig`/signature stay in sync, plus a commit-boundary projection in `useTimelineCommit.applyEdit` for `'config'`/`'pinnedShotGroups'` types; (2) updating all three clone helpers (`migrate.ts:163`, `timeline-data.ts:91`, `usePinnedShotGroups.ts:31`) to deep-copy `start`+`children`; (3) routing `useExternalDrop` shot-drop creation through `buildGroupFromClips`; (4) preserving Delete-shot's existing delete-then-unpin semantics (rev 1 had this wrong); (5) adding a new Step 11 that promotes clip-level mutations on group members (`useClipEditing` split/delete, `useKeyboardShortcuts` delete/split/Arrow keys, `useTimelineTrackManagement.moveSelectedClipsToTrack`) to group-level operations via a shared `categorizeSelection` helper; (6) explicit `isInteractionActive()` flag on `useDragCoordinator` so `usePinnedGroupSync` can defer mid-drag instead of relying on a non-existent guard; (7) added integration test at the commit boundary; (8) added `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` and `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` to the validation list. The hybrid model and settled decisions are preserved.

flags.concern: Pinned-group edit surface: Rev 2 now covers keyboard and clip-level mutation paths, but it still routes keyboard Delete on a selected group member to whole-shot deletion, which conflicts with the brief's requirement that only explicit shot-label-menu Deconstruct/Delete actions may break a group.
flags.evidence: Rev 3 fixes the 3 root causes the gate consolidated from rev 2's 16 open flags, none requiring redesign of the hybrid model: (1) **Keyboard delete/split on a group member is now a NO-OP** with an error toast, not a reroute to delete-shot semantics — the brief is explicit that only the shot-label menu may break a group. ArrowUp/Down and `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` still reroute to whole-group moves because moving doesn't break cohesion. (2) **`useExternalDrop` builds the group inside its existing loop** using the `timeOffset` accumulator and `nextEdit.duration` it already tracks at `useExternalDrop.ts:148-169` — a new `buildGroupFromChildren` primitive takes a `children` array directly so the helper doesn't need to read `workingData.config.clips` (which doesn't yet contain the new clip IDs). The selection-pinning caller uses a `buildGroupFromConfigClips` convenience wrapper. (3) **Interaction state is lifted into the editor state layer** (`useTimelineState.contexts.ts`) as a shared `interactionStateRef: { drag, resize }` ref, written by both `useClipDrag` (drag lifecycle) and `TimelineCanvas` resize handlers (`resizeSessionRef`/`pendingOpsRef` at `TimelineCanvas.tsx:737-843`), read by `usePinnedGroupSync`. Rev 2 wrongly assumed `useDragCoordinator` already tracked resizes. New Step 7 makes this explicit and Step 17 adds a sync-deferral test. All other rev-2 content (projection wiring, clone helpers, Delete-shot semantics preservation, broader edit surface) is preserved.

flags.concern: Shot-drop creation: Rev 2 names `useExternalDrop()`, but the proposed `buildGroupFromClips({ config: workingData.config, clipIds: createdClipIds, ... })` still cannot see freshly dropped clips because that path only mutates rows/meta/clipOrder until commit.

(3) **Interaction state is lifted into the editor state layer** (`useTimelineState.contexts.ts`) as a shared `interactionStateRef: { drag, resize }` ref, written by both `useClipDrag` (drag lifecycle) and `TimelineCanvas` resize handlers (`resizeSessionRef`/`pendingOpsRef` at `TimelineCanvas.tsx:737-843`), read by `usePinnedGroupSync`. Rev 2 wrongly assumed `useDragCoordinator` already tracked resizes. New Step 7 makes this explicit and Step 17 adds a sync-deferral test. All other rev-2 content (projection wiring, clone helpers, Delete-shot semantics preservation, broader edit surface) is preserved.

flags.evidence: Rev 3 fixes the 3 root causes the gate consolidated from rev 2's 16 open flags, none requiring redesign of the hybrid model: (1) **Keyboard delete/split on a group member is now a NO-OP** with an error toast, not a reroute to delete-shot semantics — the brief is explicit that only the shot-label menu may break a group. ArrowUp/Down and `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` still reroute to whole-group moves because moving doesn't break cohesion. (2) **`useExternalDrop` builds the group inside its existing loop** using the `timeOffset` accumulator and `nextEdit.duration` it already tracks at `useExternalDrop.ts:148-169` — a new `buildGroupFromChildren` primitive takes a `children` array directly so the helper doesn't need to read `workingData.config.clips` (which doesn't yet contain the new clip IDs). The selection-pinning caller uses a `buildGroupFromConfigClips` convenience wrapper. Rev 2 wrongly assumed `useDragCoordinator` already tracked resizes. New Step 7 makes this explicit and Step 17 adds a sync-deferral test.
